Issue of Share Options
Following the announcement of entering into a loan agreement on 7 April 2026, Synergia Energy Limited (
Synergia
or the
Company
) advises that it has issued 2,169,649,244 share options ("
Options
") over the Company's ordinary shares to Republic Investment Management Pte Ltd.
The Options, which have been granted in accordance with the loan agreement as announced on 07 April 2026, are exercisable at 0.0119 pence each being at a 10% premium over the prevailing share price as at 27 April 2026 and the Options expire on 27 April 2027

latest.title: Camelot CCS and Cambay PSC Update
latest.content:
Today 12:00
RNS Number : 5118I
Synergia Energy Ltd
16 June 2026
RNS Announcement
16 June 2026
AIM: SYN
Camelot CCS and Cambay PSC Update
Synergia Energy Limited (
Synergia
or the
Company
) announces the following update regarding its Camelot CCS project in the UK Southern North Sea and its Cambay PSC (WI 50%) in India.
Camelot CCS Project
Synergia has a 50% interest in the CS019 Camelot carbon storage licence in the Southern North Sea. In November 2025, the Company submitted a comprehensive legacy well integrity risk assessment report to the North Sea Transition authority
(NSTA
). The risk assessment report was reviewed on 9 June 2026 during an NSTA-hosted workshop in London.
The CS019 licence contains 13 legacy wells. The detailed abandonment data for two wells (53/01-4 and 53/01-6) have not been able to be located and the NSTA is concerned that the Mobil composite logs produced in the 1970's cannot be corroborated. The wells were abandoned with an open hole section in the Leman Sandstone and Zechstein formations and after circa 50 years, Synergia is confident these hole sections will have collapsed. Consequently, the wells cannot be re-abandoned.
In view of the uncertainty of being able to obtain a storage permit for the Leman Sandstone storage reservoir together with commercial uncertainties, the Company has decided to apply to relinquish its CS019 licence, and anticipates its 50% joint venture partner in the licence, Harbour Energy, to also request a relinquishment of its licence.
Although the Company retains its interest in the CCS sector via its Cambay CCS project in India, going forward, Synergia will continue to focus on the further development of its Cambay PSC field in India and will be assessing additional opportunities in the E&P sector.
Cambay PSC production update
Following the Company's announcement on 27 May 2026 concerning the workover program on the Cambay PSC legacy wells, the recently worked-over C-08 well has stabilised at a production level of circa 30 bopd.
Total production is currently circa 140 bopd and circa 50 MCF gas.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR") and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.
For and on behalf of Synergia Energy Limited
Roland Wessel

latest.title: Cambay PSC Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 7790F
Synergia Energy Ltd
27 May 2026
RNS Announcement
27 May 2026
AIM: SYN
Cambay PSC Update
Synergia Energy Limited (
Synergia
or the
Company
) announces the following update regarding its Cambay PSC (WI 50%) in India.
Cambay PSC Workovers
The following four legacy well workovers have been completed:
C-64: Sand build up caused the sucker rod pump ("SRP") to seize and the well was worked over to remove the sand and to optimise the SRP. The well is currently operating on a reduced pump rate and producing c. 30 bopd with a 50% water cut.
C-72: The well was re-perforated in the OSII formation and the SRP was replaced. The well is currently cleaning up the brine kill fluid.
C-19z: This well has been consistently producing small volumes of oil on natural flow. An SRP has been installed and a gas pipeline is being installed to connect gas output to the C-73 facility. Initial production rates of c. 11 bopd has since fallen to 2-3 bopd and the SRP optimisation is ongoing.
C-08: The workover operation has recently been completed and the well is currently cleaning up the brine kill fluid. The gas output is planned to be connected to the C-73 facility via a new pipeline currently under construction.
In addition to the above four wells, C-74 continues to produce c. 60 bopd and the C-77H well is producing at a rate of .08 mmscfd.
Note: All production data are gross.
C-79 new well planning
Selan and Synergia's technical teams have been in detailed discussions regarding the planned C-79 new well to be drilled into the Eocene reservoir. The discussions concern the well target which is planned to intersect the oil leg of the Eocene reservoir.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR") and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.

latest.content:
Today 07:00

Synergia Energy Ltd
12 May 2026
RNS Announcement
12 May 2026
AIM: SYN
Appointment of Non-Executive Director
The Board of Synergia Energy Limited (
Synergia
or the
Company
) announces the appointment of Stacey Britza as Non-Executive Director with immediate effect.
Stacey has been actively involved in the Company's activites for 18 years as Executive Assistant and Assistant Company Secretary. Consequently, Stacey brings valuable experience of the Company's activities and continuity.
Roland Wessel, Chief Executive Officer said:
"Stacey has been a valued employee of the Company for many years and her career has included Cairn Energy prior to her involvement with Oilex and Synergia. I look forward to Stacey's elevated involvement in the management of the Company."
For and on behalf of Synergia Energy Limited
Roland Wessel
CEO

latest.title: Cambay PSC and Board Changes
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 9083C
Synergia Energy Ltd
05 May 2026
RNS Announcement
05 May 2026
AIM: SYN
Cambay PSC and Board Changes
The Board of Synergia Energy Limited (
Synergia
or the
Company
) announces the following update regarding its Cambay PSC (50% WI) in India and Board structure update.
Cambay PSC Update
Oil production for April 2026 averaged 148 bopd primarily from two wells (C-64 and C-74) that were worked over in October 2025. Gas production for the month averaged 39 MCF/day from the C-77H well.
The Company is in discussion with its joint venture partner, Antelopus Selan Energy Ltd ("Selan") regarding the proposed new well (C-79) to be drilled later this year into the Eocene formation.
Board Changes
Mark Bolton, Non-Executive Director, is stepping down from the Synergia Board of Directors after several years of valuable service. Mark will continue to assist the Company under an advisory contract. The Company will look to appoint Stacey Britza, Synergia's Assistant Secretary, as a Non-Executive Director, and a further announcement will be made in due course following her appointment.
Roland Wessel, Chief Executive Officer said:
"
Mark Bolton has helped steer the Company through challenging years and we are grateful for his contribution and wish him every success for the future
."
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR") and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.

company_30s.what_they_do: SYN.L operates in the mining sector, mainly exploring and developing the Cambay Production Share Company (PSC) for mineral extraction. The company’s core activities revolve around drilling, feasibility studies, and securing permits to bring a mining project to life.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The Cambay PSC sits in a region rich in copper and other base metals, offering a potential source of supply for the growing global demand for industrial metals. If the project succeeds, it could provide a new foothold for the UK’s mining industry and offer investors a stake in a high‑growth commodity.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still in the early exploration phase, relying on short‑term financing and a modest loan to cover operating costs. It has not yet produced any revenue, and its cash burn remains a concern. The share price is near zero, reflecting the market’s lack of confidence in a near‑term turnaround.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy with corporate governance moves – appointing a non‑executive director, issuing share options, and securing a £0.7 m loan. These steps show an attempt to shore up the balance sheet and keep the PSC project alive.
Production updates and a correction notice on 6 March indicate that the team is still trying to refine the technical data for the Cambay field, but no breakthrough has been announced.
Overall, the narrative is one of survival and incremental financing, with no clear sign that the project is moving from the exploratory to the development stage.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Indifferent silence – the community is largely silent, with sporadic, mostly negative chatter.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No bullish voices have emerged. The lack of any positive sentiment is a clear sign that the market sees no imminent upside.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears argue that the company’s repeated reliance on short‑term funding and lack of production data points to a high likelihood of continued decline or even liquidation.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has remained flat over the past weeks, with no evidence of a shift toward optimism. The community’s silence suggests that investors are waiting for a decisive catalyst that never materialised.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no recent search interest, which means that any news that does hit could create a surprise rally.

near_term_catalysts: A positive production update from the Cambay PSC that shows a viable ore body and a realistic production schedule.
Closing of a new funding round or a strategic partnership with a larger mining operator.
A regulatory decision that clears the PSC for development, such as a final environmental approval.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume coupled with a price breakout above the recent support level around the current trading range. Any announcement that the PSC will enter a development phase or that a new investor has committed capital should be treated as a potential trigger.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a potential capitulation cycle. The lack of a clear mean‑reversion signal and the absence of a deep crash (we don’t know the exact drawdown) make it difficult to gauge how far the price has fallen. The APEX score is not high enough to confirm a tight compression that would signal an imminent bounce.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the evidence for a contrarian upside is weak, and the risk of further decline is high.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company secures a new investment or confirms a viable production plan, the price could recover to the mid‑range of its 52‑week spread. A 50‑pence rebound would represent a 100 % return on the current trading level, but this hinges on a single, unproven catalyst.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company has not demonstrated any clear path to revenue and has repeatedly relied on short‑term financing. Without a breakthrough in the Cambay PSC, the share price is likely to stay near zero or drop further if a liquidation or delisting becomes inevitable.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is capped at a modest rebound to the 52‑week high, while downside remains unlimited as the share could become worthless if the project fails or the company is wound down.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter only if a credible funding announcement or production milestone is confirmed. Allocate a small portion of the portfolio (no more than 2 % of capital) and set a tight stop just below the current price to protect against a continued slide.
playbook.if_watching: Keep an eye on the 6 March production update and any news of regulatory approvals. A sudden volume surge or a price move above the recent resistance level would be the signal to consider a position.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy filings, but if the company fails to secure a new investor within the next 90 days, consider exiting the position.

ai_insights.bottom_line: SYN.L is a stalled mining explorer with dead market interest and no clear catalyst; the contrarian upside is limited and the risk of a further decline remains high.
